seo_metadata.title: llama_index by run-llama - Explain, Architecture & Alternatives
seo_metadata.description: LlamaIndex is the leading document agent and OCR platform

content.what_it_does:
llama_index is a comprehensive Python-based software project developed and maintained by run-llama. The primary focus of this repository is to provide robust solutions in the domain of: LlamaIndex is the leading document agent and OCR platform It heavily intersects with key technological areas including agents, application, data, fine-tuning, framework. According to the project documentation: LlamaIndex OSS (by LlamaIndex) is an open-source framework to build agentic applications. **Parse** is our enterprise platform for agentic OCR, parsing, extraction, indexing and more. You can use LlamaParse with this framework or on its own; see LlamaParse below for signup and product links. Building with LlamaIndex typically involves working with LlamaIndex core and a chosen set of integrations (or plugins). There are two ways to start building with LlamaIndex in
Python: 1. **Starter**: `llama-index`. A starter Python package that includes core LlamaIndex as well as a selection of integrations.
key_features: Built natively with Python for optimal performance and ecosystem compatibility.
Open source architecture allowing for extensive community contributions on GitHub.
Supports and implements modern paradigms such as agents, application, data.
Provides a systematic approach to solving standard domain-specific challenges.
content.architecture_overview: The llama_index repository is organized following industry standard Python conventions to ensure maintainability and scalability. The codebase is logically grouped around its core functionalities, offering a main entry point and modular components. It leverages modern software package management systems, explicitly relying on configurations found in pyproject.toml. This indicates a canonical Python environment setup where dependencies and scripts are isolated and managed robustly.
content.how_to_run:
To deploy or interact with llama_index locally, you should typically follow these standard sequential steps:
1. Clone the repository directly from GitHub: `git clone https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index.git`
2. Navigate into the freshly cloned directory via your terminal.
3. Carefully inspect the root README.md file for highly specific installation prerequisites or build scripts.
4. Execute the primary run command or entry script to instantiate the service or utilize the library.
